clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Guide referral decisions for adults with positive screening tests for secondary hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: After positive screening test for secondary hypertension (renal artery stenosis, primary aldosteronism, pheochromocytoma, etc.)
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Referral to hypertension specialist for diagnostic confirmation and treatment

secondaryHypertensionType.validation: Must identify specific form (renal, endocrine, etc.)

validationRules: Positive screening test must be documented
Secondary hypertension type should be specified when possible

rules: Confirm positive screening test for secondary hypertension
Identify specific form of secondary hypertension suspected
Determine appropriate specialist type (nephrologist, endocrinologist, cardiologist)
Generate referral recommendation based on COR 2a (MODERATE strength)
Apply LOE C-EO (expert opinion based consensus)
Coordinate with primary care for comanagement

categories.name: LOE C-EO
categories.source: Consensus of expert opinion based on clinical experience

recommendation.description: In adults who have a positive screening test for a form of secondary hypertension, referral to a clinician who has expertise in that form of hypertension is reasonable for diagnostic confirmation and treatment.

loeDescription.description: Level C-EO: Consensus of expert opinion based on clinical experience

actionableStep.description: Refer to hypertension specialist for diagnostic confirmation

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Emergency referral rather than routine specialist referral

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Refer to hypertension specialist within 4-6 weeks for diagnostic confirmation
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Follow up after specialist evaluation (typically 1-3 months)
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor blood pressure while awaiting specialist appointment
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if referral not completed within recommended timeframe
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log screening test results, referral order, and specialist appointment scheduled
